question_text: The second condition defines vj = yj −xj and uj = 2xj. The goal is to minimize the sum of squared errors for the linear regression model vj = γuj + ϵj. We substitute the expressions for vj and uj into the formula for the least-squares estimator: ˆγ = i=1 uivi i=1 u2 Substitute vi = yi −xi and ui = 2xi: ˆγ = i=1(2xi)(yi −xi) i=1(2xi)2 Simplify the equation: ˆγ = 2 Pn i=1 xi(yi −xi) 4 Pn i=1 x2 i=1 xi(yi −xi) 2 Pn i=1 x2 Now, expand yi = βxi + ϵi and substitute ˆβ = 10: ˆγ = i=1 xi (βxi + ϵi −xi) 2 Pn i=1 x2 Since ˆβ = 10, this simplifies to: ˆγ = i=1 xi (10xi −xi) 2 Pn i=1 x2 i=1 xi(9xi) 2 Pn i=1 x2 = 9 Pn i=1 x2 2 Pn i=1 x2 Thus, ˆγ = 9 2 = 4.5. Therefore, the value of ˆγ is 5. Quick Tip The least-squares estimate for a linear model can be used to minimize the sum of squared errors. When dealing with transformations of the variables, apply the same logic to find the optimal estimator for the new model.
